36	from llama_index.core.utils import resolve_binary

39	class MessageRole(str, Enum):
40	    """Message role."""
41	
42	    SYSTEM = "system"
43	    DEVELOPER = "developer"
44	    USER = "user"
45	    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
46	    FUNCTION = "function"
47	    TOOL = "tool"
48	    CHATBOT = "chatbot"
49	    MODEL = "model"
50	
51	
52	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
53	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
54	    text: str
55	
56	
57	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
58	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
59	    image: bytes | None = None
60	    path: FilePath | None = None
61	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
62	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
63	    detail: str | None = None
64	
65	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
66	    @classmethod
67	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl | None) -> AnyUrl | None:
68	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
69	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
70	            return url
71	        if url is None:
72	            return None
73	
74	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
75	
76	    @model_validator(mode="after")
77	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
78	        """
79	        Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
80	
81	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
82	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
83	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
84	        """
85	        if not self.image:
86	            if not self.image_mimetype:
87	                path = self.path or self.url
88	                if path:
89	                    suffix = Path(str(path)).suffix.replace(".", "") or None
90	                    mimetype = filetype.get_type(ext=suffix)
91	                    if mimetype and str(mimetype.mime).startswith("image/"):
92	                        self.image_mimetype = str(mimetype.mime)
93	
94	            return self
95	
96	        try:
97	            # Check if self.image is already base64 encoded.
98	            # b64decode() can succeed on random binary data, so we
99	            # pass verify=True to make sure it's not a false positive
100	            decoded_img = base64.b64decode(self.image, validate=True)
101	        except BinasciiError:
102	            decoded_img = self.image
103	            self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
104	
105	        self._guess_mimetype(decoded_img)
106	        return self
107	
108	    def _guess_mimetype(self, img_data: bytes) -> None:
109	        if not self.image_mimetype:
110	            guess = filetype.guess(img_data)
111	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
112	
113	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
114	        """
115	        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
116	
117	        Args:
118	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
119	
120	        """
121	        return resolve_binary(
122	            raw_bytes=self.image,
123	            path=self.path,
124	            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
125	            as_base64=as_base64,
126	        )
127	
128	
129	class AudioBlock(BaseModel):
130	    block_type: Literal["audio"] = "audio"
131	    audio: bytes | None = None
132	    path: FilePath | None = None
133	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
134	    format: str | None = None
135	
136	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
137	    @classmethod
138	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
139	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
140	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
141	            return url
142	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
143	
144	    @model_validator(mode="after")
145	    def audio_to_base64(self) -> Self:
146	        """
147	        Store the audio as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
148	
149	        In case the model was built passing audio data but without a mimetype,
150	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
151	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
152	        """
153	        if not self.audio:
154	            return self
155	
156	        try:
157	            # Check if audio is already base64 encoded
158	            decoded_audio = base64.b64decode(self.audio)
159	        except Exception:
160	            decoded_audio = self.audio
161	            # Not base64 - encode it
162	            self.audio = base64.b64encode(self.audio)
163	
164	        self._guess_format(decoded_audio)
165	
166	        return self
167	
168	    def _guess_format(self, audio_data: bytes) -> None:
169	        if not self.format:
170	            guess = filetype.guess(audio_data)
171	            self.format = guess.extension if guess else None
172	
173	    def resolve_audio(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
174	        """
175	        Resolve an audio such that PIL can read it.
176	
177	        Args:
178	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved audio should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
179	
180	        """
181	        return resolve_binary(
182	            raw_bytes=self.audio,
183	            path=self.path,
184	            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
185	            as_base64=as_base64,
186	        )

188	class DocumentBlock(BaseModel):
189	    block_type: Literal["document"] = "document"
190	    data: Optional[bytes] = None
191	    path: Optional[Union[FilePath | str]] = None
192	    url: Optional[str] = None
193	    title: Optional[str] = None
194	    document_mimetype: Optional[str] = None
195	
196	    @model_validator(mode="after")
197	    def document_validation(self) -> Self:
198	        self.document_mimetype = self.document_mimetype or self._guess_mimetype()
199	
200	        if not self.title:
201	            self.title = "input_document"
202	
203	        # skip data validation if it's not provided
204	        if not self.data:
205	            return self
206	
207	        try:
208	            decoded_document = base64.b64decode(self.data, validate=True)
209	        except BinasciiError:
210	            self.data = base64.b64encode(self.data)
211	
212	        return self
213	
214	    def resolve_document(self) -> BytesIO:
215	        """
216	        Resolve a document such that it is represented by a BufferIO object.
217	        """
218	        return resolve_binary(
219	            raw_bytes=self.data,
220	            path=self.path,
221	            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
222	            as_base64=False,
223	        )
224	
225	    def _get_b64_string(self, data_buffer: BytesIO) -> str:
226	        """
227	        Get base64-encoded string from a BytesIO buffer.
228	        """
229	        data = data_buffer.read()
230	        return base64.b64encode(data).decode("utf-8")
231	
232	    def _get_b64_bytes(self, data_buffer: BytesIO) -> bytes:
233	        """
234	        Get base64-encoded bytes from a BytesIO buffer.
235	        """
236	        data = data_buffer.read()
237	        return base64.b64encode(data)
238	
239	    def guess_format(self) -> str | None:
240	        path = self.path or self.url
241	        if not path:
242	            return None
243	
244	        return Path(str(path)).suffix.replace(".", "")
245	
246	    def _guess_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
247	        if self.data:
248	            guess = filetype.guess(self.data)
249	            return str(guess.mime) if guess else None
250	
251	        suffix = self.guess_format()
252	        if not suffix:
253	            return None
254	
255	        guess = filetype.get_type(ext=suffix)
256	        return str(guess.mime) if guess else None
257	
258	ContentBlock = Annotated[
259	    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock, AudioBlock, DocumentBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
260	]
261	
262	class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
263	    """Chat message."""
264	
265	    role: MessageRole = MessageRole.USER
266	    additional_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
267	    blocks: list[ContentBlock] = Field(default_factory=list)
268	
269	    def __init__(self, /, content: Any | None = None, **data: Any) -> None:
270	        """
271	        Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
272	
273	        If content was passed and contained text, store a single TextBlock.
274	        If content was passed and it was a list, assume it's a list of content blocks and store it.
275	        """
276	        if content is not None:
277	            if isinstance(content, str):
278	                data["blocks"] = [TextBlock(text=content)]
279	            elif isinstance(content, list):
280	                data["blocks"] = content
281	
282	        super().__init__(**data)
283	
284	    @model_validator(mode="after")
285	    def legacy_additional_kwargs_image(self) -> Self:
286	        """
287	        Provided for backward compatibility.
288	
289	        If `additional_kwargs` contains an `images` key, assume the value is a list
290	        of ImageDocument and convert them into image blocks.
291	        """
292	        if documents := self.additional_kwargs.get("images"):
293	            documents = cast(list[ImageDocument], documents)
294	            for doc in documents:
295	                img_base64_bytes = doc.resolve_image(as_base64=True).read()
296	                self.blocks.append(ImageBlock(image=img_base64_bytes))
297	        return self
298	
299	    @property
300	    def content(self) -> str | None:
301	        """
302	        Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
303	
304	        Returns:
305	            The cumulative content of the TextBlock blocks, None if there are none.
306	
307	        """
308	        content_strs = []
309	        for block in self.blocks:
310	            if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
311	                content_strs.append(block.text)
312	
313	        return "\n".join(content_strs) or None
314	
315	    @content.setter
316	    def content(self, content: str) -> None:
317	        """
318	        Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
319	
320	        Raises:
321	            ValueError: if blocks contains more than a block, or a block that's not TextBlock.
322	
323	        """
324	        if not self.blocks:
325	            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
326	        elif len(self.blocks) == 1 and isinstance(self.blocks[0], TextBlock):
327	            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
328	        else:
329	            raise ValueError(
330	                "ChatMessage contains multiple blocks, use 'ChatMessage.blocks' instead."
331	            )
332	
333	    def __str__(self) -> str:
334	        return f"{self.role.value}: {self.content}"
335	
336	    @classmethod
337	    def from_str(
338	        cls,
339	        content: str,
340	        role: Union[MessageRole, str] = MessageRole.USER,
341	        **kwargs: Any,
342	    ) -> Self:
343	        if isinstance(role, str):
344	            role = MessageRole(role)
345	        return cls(role=role, blocks=[TextBlock(text=content)], **kwargs)
346	
347	    def _recursive_serialization(self, value: Any) -> Any:
348	        if isinstance(value, BaseModel):
349	            value.model_rebuild()  # ensures all fields are initialized and serializable
350	            return value.model_dump()  # type: ignore
351	        if isinstance(value, dict):
352	            return {
353	                key: self._recursive_serialization(value)
354	                for key, value in value.items()
355	            }
356	        if isinstance(value, list):
357	            return [self._recursive_serialization(item) for item in value]
358	        return value
359	
360	    @field_serializer("additional_kwargs", check_fields=False)
361	    def serialize_additional_kwargs(self, value: Any, _info: Any) -> Any:
362	        return self._recursive_serialization(value)
363	
364	
365	class LogProb(BaseModel):
366	    """LogProb of a token."""
367	
368	    token: str = Field(default_factory=str)
369	    logprob: float = Field(default_factory=float)
370	    bytes: List[int] = Field(default_factory=list)
371	
372	
373	# ===== Generic Model Output - Chat =====
374	class ChatResponse(BaseModel):
375	    """Chat response."""
376	
377	    message: ChatMessage
378	    raw: Optional[Any] = None
379	    delta: Optional[str] = None
380	    logprobs: Optional[List[List[LogProb]]] = None
381	    additional_kwargs: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
382	
383	    def __str__(self) -> str:
384	        return str(self.message)
385	
386	
387	ChatResponseGen = Generator[ChatResponse, None, None]
388	ChatResponseAsyncGen = AsyncGenerator[ChatResponse, None]
389	
390	
391	# ===== Generic Model Output - Completion =====
392	class CompletionResponse(BaseModel):
393	    """
394	    Completion response.
395	
396	    Fields:
397	        text: Text content of the response if not streaming, or if streaming,
398	            the current extent of streamed text.
399	        additional_kwargs: Additional information on the response(i.e. token
400	            counts, function calling information).
401	        raw: Optional raw JSON that was parsed to populate text, if relevant.
402	        delta: New text that just streamed in (only relevant when streaming).
403	    """
404	
405	    text: str
406	    additional_kwargs: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
407	    raw: Optional[Any] = None
408	    logprobs: Optional[List[List[LogProb]]] = None
409	    delta: Optional[str] = None
410	
411	    def __str__(self) -> str:
412	        return self.text
413	
414	
415	CompletionResponseGen = Generator[CompletionResponse, None, None]
416	CompletionResponseAsyncGen = AsyncGenerator[CompletionResponse, None]
417	
418	
419	class LLMMetadata(BaseModel):
420	    model_config = ConfigDict(
421	        protected_namespaces=("pydantic_model_",), arbitrary_types_allowed=True
422	    )
423	    context_window: int = Field(
424	        default=DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW,
425	        description=(
426	            "Total number of tokens the model can be input and output for one response."
427	        ),
428	    )
429	    num_output: int = Field(
430	        default=DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS,
431	        description="Number of tokens the model can output when generating a response.",
432	    )
433	    is_chat_model: bool = Field(
434	        default=False,
435	        description=(
436	            "Set True if the model exposes a chat interface (i.e. can be passed a"
437	            " sequence of messages, rather than text), like OpenAI's"
438	            " /v1/chat/completions endpoint."
439	        ),
440	    )
441	    is_function_calling_model: bool = Field(
442	        default=False,
443	        # SEE: https://openai.com/blog/function-calling-and-other-api-updates
444	        description=(
445	            "Set True if the model supports function calling messages, similar to"
446	            " OpenAI's function calling API. For example, converting 'Email Anya to"
447	            " see if she wants to get coffee next Friday' to a function call like"
448	            " `send_email(to: string, body: string)`."
449	        ),
450	    )
451	    model_name: str = Field(
452	        default="unknown",
453	        description=(
454	            "The model's name used for logging, testing, and sanity checking. For some"
455	            " models this can be automatically discerned. For other models, like"
456	            " locally loaded models, this must be manually specified."
457	        ),
458	    )
459	    system_role: MessageRole = Field(
460	        default=MessageRole.SYSTEM,
461	        description="The role this specific LLM provider"
462	        "expects for system prompt. E.g. 'SYSTEM' for OpenAI, 'CHATBOT' for Cohere",
463	    )

input.old_string:
def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        return resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.image,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
input.new_string:
def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.image,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_image returned zero bytes")
        return buffer

from __future__ import annotations

import base64
import filetype
from binascii import Error as BinasciiError
from enum import Enum
from io import BytesIO
from pathlib import Path
from typing import (
    Annotated,
    Any,
    AsyncGenerator,
    Generator,
    List,
    Literal,
    Optional,
    Union,
    cast,
)

import filetype
from typing_extensions import Self

from llama_index.core.bridge.pydantic import (
    AnyUrl,
    BaseModel,
    ConfigDict,
    Field,
    FilePath,
    field_serializer,
    field_validator,
    model_validator,
)
from llama_index.core.constants import DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW, DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS
from llama_index.core.schema import ImageDocument
from llama_index.core.utils import resolve_binary


class MessageRole(str, Enum):
    """Message role."""

    SYSTEM = "system"
    DEVELOPER = "developer"
    USER = "user"
    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
    FUNCTION = "function"
    TOOL = "tool"
    CHATBOT = "chatbot"
    MODEL = "model"


class TextBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
    text: str


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl | None) -> AnyUrl | None:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        if url is None:
            return None

        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """
        Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            if not self.image_mimetype:
                path = self.path or self.url
                if path:
                    suffix = Path(str(path)).suffix.replace(".", "") or None
                    mimetype = filetype.get_type(ext=suffix)
                    if mimetype and str(mimetype.mime).startswith("image/"):
                        self.image_mimetype = str(mimetype.mime)

            return self

        try:
            # Check if self.image is already base64 encoded.
            # b64decode() can succeed on random binary data, so we
            # pass verify=True to make sure it's not a false positive
            decoded_img = base64.b64decode(self.image, validate=True)
        except BinasciiError:
            decoded_img = self.image
            self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)

        self._guess_mimetype(decoded_img)
        return self

    def _guess_mimetype(self, img_data: bytes) -> None:
        if not self.image_mimetype:
            guess = filetype.guess(img_data)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.image,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_image returned zero bytes")
        return buffer


class AudioBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["audio"] = "audio"
    audio: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    format: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def audio_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """
        Store the audio as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing audio data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.audio:
            return self

        try:
            # Check if audio is already base64 encoded
            decoded_audio = base64.b64decode(self.audio)
        except Exception:
            decoded_audio = self.audio
            # Not base64 - encode it
            self.audio = base64.b64encode(self.audio)

        self._guess_format(decoded_audio)

        return self

    def _guess_format(self, audio_data: bytes) -> None:
        if not self.format:
            guess = filetype.guess(audio_data)
            self.format = guess.extension if guess else None

    def resolve_audio(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an audio such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved audio should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.audio,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_audio returned zero bytes")
        return buffer

class DocumentBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["document"] = "document"
    data: Optional[bytes] = None
    path: Optional[Union[FilePath | str]] = None
    url: Optional[str] = None
    title: Optional[str] = None
    document_mimetype: Optional[str] = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def document_validation(self) -> Self:
        self.document_mimetype = self.document_mimetype or self._guess_mimetype()

        if not self.title:
            self.title = "input_document"

        # skip data validation if it's not provided
        if not self.data:
            return self

        try:
            decoded_document = base64.b64decode(self.data, validate=True)
        except BinasciiError:
            self.data = base64.b64encode(self.data)

        return self

    def resolve_document(self) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve a document such that it is represented by a BufferIO object.
        """
        return resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.data,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=False,
        )

    def _get_b64_string(self, data_buffer: BytesIO) -> str:
        """
        Get base64-encoded string from a BytesIO buffer.
        """
        data = data_buffer.read()
        return base64.b64encode(data).decode("utf-8")

    def _get_b64_bytes(self, data_buffer: BytesIO) -> bytes:
        """
        Get base64-encoded bytes from a BytesIO buffer.
        """
        data = data_buffer.read()
        return base64.b64encode(data)

    def guess_format(self) -> str | None:
        path = self.path or self.url
        if not path:
            return None

        return Path(str(path)).suffix.replace(".", "")

    def _guess_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        if self.data:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.data)
            return str(guess.mime) if guess else None

        suffix = self.guess_format()
        if not suffix:
            return None

        guess = filetype.get_type(ext=suffix)
        return str(guess.mime) if guess else None

ContentBlock = Annotated[
    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock, AudioBlock, DocumentBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
]

class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
    """Chat message."""

    role: MessageRole = MessageRole.USER
    additional_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
    blocks: list[ContentBlock] = Field(default_factory=list)

    def __init__(self, /, content: Any | None = None, **data: Any) -> None:
        """
        Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.

        If content was passed and contained text, store a single TextBlock.
        If content was passed and it was a list, assume it's a list of content blocks and store it.
        """
        if content is not None:
            if isinstance(content, str):
                data["blocks"] = [TextBlock(text=content)]
            elif isinstance(content, list):
                data["blocks"] = content

        super().__init__(**data)

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def legacy_additional_kwargs_image(self) -> Self:
        """
        Provided for backward compatibility.

        If `additional_kwargs` contains an `images` key, assume the value is a list
        of ImageDocument and convert them into image blocks.
        """
        if documents := self.additional_kwargs.get("images"):
            documents = cast(list[ImageDocument], documents)
            for doc in documents:
                img_base64_bytes = doc.resolve_image(as_base64=True).read()
                self.blocks.append(ImageBlock(image=img_base64_bytes))
        return self

    @property
    def content(self) -> str | None:
        """
        Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.

        Returns:
            The cumulative content of the TextBlock blocks, None if there are none.

        """
        content_strs = []
        for block in self.blocks:
            if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
                content_strs.append(block.text)

        return "\n".join(content_strs) or None

    @content.setter
    def content(self, content: str) -> None:
        """
        Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.

        Raises:
            ValueError: if blocks contains more than a block, or a block that's not TextBlock.

        """
        if not self.blocks:
            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
        elif len(self.blocks) == 1 and isinstance(self.blocks[0], TextBlock):
            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
        else:
            raise ValueError(
                "ChatMessage contains multiple blocks, use 'ChatMessage.blocks' instead."
            )

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return f"{self.role.value}: {self.content}"

    @classmethod
    def from_str(
        cls,
        content: str,
        role: Union[MessageRole, str] = MessageRole.USER,
        **kwargs: Any,
    ) -> Self:
        if isinstance(role, str):
            role = MessageRole(role)
        return cls(role=role, blocks=[TextBlock(text=content)], **kwargs)

    def _recursive_serialization(self, value: Any) -> Any:
        if isinstance(value, BaseModel):
            value.model_rebuild()  # ensures all fields are initialized and serializable
            return value.model_dump()  # type: ignore
        if isinstance(value, dict):
            return {
                key: self._recursive_serialization(value)
                for key, value in value.items()
            }
        if isinstance(value, list):
            return [self._recursive_serialization(item) for item in value]
        return value

    @field_serializer("additional_kwargs", check_fields=False)
    def serialize_additional_kwargs(self, value: Any, _info: Any) -> Any:
        return self._recursive_serialization(value)


class LogProb(BaseModel):
    """LogProb of a token."""

    token: str = Field(default_factory=str)
    logprob: float = Field(default_factory=float)
    bytes: List[int] = Field(default_factory=list)


# ===== Generic Model Output - Chat =====
class ChatResponse(BaseModel):
    """Chat response."""

    message: ChatMessage
    raw: Optional[Any] = None
    delta: Optional[str] = None
    logprobs: Optional[List[List[LogProb]]] = None
    additional_kwargs: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return str(self.message)


ChatResponseGen = Generator[ChatResponse, None, None]
ChatResponseAsyncGen = AsyncGenerator[ChatResponse, None]


# ===== Generic Model Output - Completion =====
class CompletionResponse(BaseModel):
    """
    Completion response.

    Fields:
        text: Text content of the response if not streaming, or if streaming,
            the current extent of streamed text.
        additional_kwargs: Additional information on the response(i.e. token
            counts, function calling information).
        raw: Optional raw JSON that was parsed to populate text, if relevant.
        delta: New text that just streamed in (only relevant when streaming).
    """

    text: str
    additional_kwargs: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
    raw: Optional[Any] = None
    logprobs: Optional[List[List[LogProb]]] = None
    delta: Optional[str] = None

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return self.text


CompletionResponseGen = Generator[CompletionResponse, None, None]
CompletionResponseAsyncGen = AsyncGenerator[CompletionResponse, None]


class LLMMetadata(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(
        protected_namespaces=("pydantic_model_",), arbitrary_types_allowed=True
    )
    context_window: int = Field(
        default=DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW,
        description=(
            "Total number of tokens the model can be input and output for one response."
        ),
    )
    num_output: int = Field(
        default=DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS,
        description="Number of tokens the model can output when generating a response.",
    )
    is_chat_model: bool = Field(
        default=False,
        description=(
            "Set True if the model exposes a chat interface (i.e. can be passed a"
            " sequence of messages, rather than text), like OpenAI's"
            " /v1/chat/completions endpoint."
        ),
    )
    is_function_calling_model: bool = Field(
        default=False,
        # SEE: https://openai.com/blog/function-calling-and-other-api-updates
        description=(
            "Set True if the model supports function calling messages, similar to"
            " OpenAI's function calling API. For example, converting 'Email Anya to"
            " see if she wants to get coffee next Friday' to a function call like"
            " `send_email(to: string, body: string)`."
        ),
    )
    model_name: str = Field(
        default="unknown",
        description=(
            "The model's name used for logging, testing, and sanity checking. For some"
            " models this can be automatically discerned. For other models, like"
            " locally loaded models, this must be manually specified."
        ),
    )
    system_role: MessageRole = Field(
        default=MessageRole.SYSTEM,
        description="The role this specific LLM provider"
        "expects for system prompt. E.g. 'SYSTEM' for OpenAI, 'CHATBOT' for Cohere",
    )

content.text: The diff is clean and contains only the intended changes to `types.py`. Let me check agent2's messages once more before finishing.

Confirmed no conflict — agent2 is adding new `_with_size` methods after each `resolve_*` method, while my changes modify the body of the existing `resolve_*` methods themselves. Since each agent submits its own independent patch, this doesn't require further coordination.

**Summary**: Modified `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py` so `ImageBlock.resolve_image`, `AudioBlock.resolve_audio`, and `DocumentBlock.resolve_document` each check the resolved `BytesIO` buffer's size (seek to end, `tell()`, seek back to 0) and raise `ValueError` if it's zero bytes. Verified via direct script testing that empty content now raises the correct error message and non-empty content still resolves correctly. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
